brief_document.title: aion-worker-typescript: defineActivity, context, error classification, and Worker
brief_document.purpose: Give the TypeScript author the native surface: defineActivity<I, O> with generic typed I/O, a ctx for heartbeat and cancellation, RetryableError/TerminalError classification, and a Worker that serves until graceful shutdown. This completes the TypeScript SDK.
brief_document.task: Implement activity.ts (defineActivity<I, O>, JSON Payload codec, registry), context.ts (heartbeat + cancellation observation), errors.ts (RetryableError / TerminalError and unclassified-defaults-to-retryable), and worker.ts (Worker class + run with graceful drain). Fill the dispatch seam AR-009's loop calls.

requirements.title: defineActivity and JSON codec
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide defineActivity<I, O>(name, handler) registering a typed activity whose generic types drive JSON Payload encode/decode at the boundary. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT change the content type on round-trip.
acceptance: activity.ts defines defineActivity<I, O>(name: string, handler: (input: I, ctx: ActivityContext) => Promise<O>) and registers it in a registry keyed by name.
Input Payload decodes to I and output O encodes to a Payload via JSON, preserving the content-type tag (CO7).
Registering two activities under the same name throws a clear error at registration.
The dispatch seam (called by AR-009's loop) decodes input, invokes the handler with a ctx, and encodes the result.
A test defines an activity, dispatches an encoded input through the registry, and asserts the output Payload decodes to the expected value with matching types.

requirements.title: Activity context: heartbeat and cancellation
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL pass a ctx to each handler exposing heartbeat(detail) and cancellation observation (isCancelled() and an awaitable cancelled()). THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT heartbeat on the handler's behalf and SHALL NOT forcibly cancel the handler.
acceptance: context.ts defines ActivityContext with activityId, attempt, heartbeat(detail?) routing to the session, isCancelled(): boolean, and cancelled(): Promise<void>.
An engine cancellation flips the ctx cancellation flag; the handler promise is not rejected/aborted by the SDK (cooperative only, per D5 / CO9).
heartbeat sends only on explicit call (per D4); no timer auto-heartbeats.
A test signals cancellation mid-handler and asserts isCancelled() becomes true while the handler is allowed to resolve on its own.

requirements.title: Error classification
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide RetryableError and TerminalError classes for handlers to throw. IF an unclassified value is thrown from a handler, THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL classify the failure as retryable and SHALL log it as unclassified.
acceptance: errors.ts defines RetryableError and TerminalError; throwing one yields the corresponding wire classification (aligned with aion-core ActivityError per CO8).
An unclassified throw is reported as a retryable failure and logged at warn level as unclassified (never silent, per D6 / CO8).
The reported failure carries the error message and, where available, a detail Payload.
Tests assert: RetryableError → retryable wire failure; TerminalError → terminal wire failure; a thrown Error → retryable wire failure plus an emitted unclassified warning.

requirements.title: Worker class, run, and graceful drain
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide a Worker class that registers activities and, on await worker.run(), connects and serves until graceful shutdown, draining in-flight activities before returning.
acceptance: worker.ts defines a Worker taking a WorkerConfig and registered activities; await worker.run() connects (AR-009), serves, and on a shutdown signal (e.g. an AbortSignal) stops pulling new tasks and drains in-flight ones before returning.
Cooperative cancellation of in-flight activities on shutdown is offered via ctx (per R2) but not forced.
A test runs a Worker against the fake session, feeds a slow task, triggers shutdown mid-task, and asserts run() resolves only after the in-flight task completes.
An end-to-end test against the fake session defines an activity, serves a task, and asserts the recorded completion — proving the full TS SDK path.

boundaries: SHALL NOT re-implement the session/loop/reconnect — those are AR-009; this brief fills the dispatch seam and the author surface.
SHALL NOT change the content type of a payload on round-trip (CO7).
SHALL NOT forcibly cancel a running handler (CO9).
verification: eslint passes with no disabled rules in new code (CO2).
vitest passes, including classification, cancellation, and graceful-drain tests.
The package builds and an end-to-end fake-session test proves the full TS SDK path.
No // @ts-ignore, // @ts-nocheck, or eslint-disable in new code.

checklist.text: defineActivity<I, O>(name, handler) registers a typed activity; the generic types drive Payload encode/decode (JSON baseline) at the boundary.
A ctx object passed to the handler exposes heartbeat(detail) and cancellation observation (isCancelled() and an awaitable cancelled()).
RetryableError and TerminalError classes classify failures; an unclassified throw escaping a handler defaults to retryable and is logged as unclassified (never silent).
A Worker class registers activities and await worker.run() connects, serves, and shuts down gracefully draining in-flight activities.

stories.text: As a TypeScript activity author, I want defineActivity<I, O> with generic input/output types so that my handler is type-checked and serialisation is handled at the boundary.
As a worker operator, I want a worker to shut down gracefully, draining in-flight activities before exiting so that a deploy does not abandon running work.

constraints.text: unsafe_code = deny in aion-worker (Rust). No unsafe.
No lint-bypass directives. Rust: no #[allow]/#[expect]/#[ignore]. Python: no blanket # type: ignore or # noqa. TS: no // @ts-ignore / // @ts-nocheck / eslint-disable. Fix the code instead.
mod.rs/lib.rs (Rust) and __init__.py/index.ts (Python/TS) are declarations and re-exports only — no logic.
500-line file limit (excluding tests/comments/whitespace) in every language.

The wire contract is owned by aion-proto (AW). This cluster references wire types and RPCs; it does not define or alter them. Where AW and this design disagree on a name or shape, AW wins and the SDK adapts.

The activity-execution machinery (loop, dispatch, context, classification, reconnect) must be testable without a live engine, against an in-memory fake session/endpoint. Tests needing a real engine use a runtime env-gate (AION_WORKER_TEST_URL) and skip with a logged line when unset.
Payload encode/decode uses the content-type tag from aion-core's Payload; JSON is the baseline codec present in all three SDKs. An SDK must not silently change content type on round-trip.

Failure classification (retryable vs terminal) is explicit at the author boundary in all three SDKs and maps onto the wire identically (D6). Unclassified-defaults-to-retryable is logged, never silent.
Cancellation is cooperative only (D5). No SDK forcibly kills a running handler's thread/task.
Concurrency limits are operator-configured (D9). No SDK hardcodes a concurrency cap or other tunable as an assumed default.
Reconnect must re-report unacknowledged results before serving new tasks (D7). Completed work is never dropped on a disconnect.

Per-language toolchains gate every brief: Rust cargo clippy -D warnings + cargo fmt --check + cargo test; Python ruff + mypy --strict + pytest; TS tsc --noEmit strict + eslint + vitest. All relevant gates pass clean before a brief lands.

resolved_context.intention: This cluster is how Tier 3 work — work that cannot run on beamr — gets done. A workflow dispatches an activity; a worker written in Python, TypeScript, or Rust, connected to the engine and holding a function registered under that activity's name, receives the task, runs it in its own runtime, and returns the result for the engine to record. On replay the recorded result comes back without re-execution. Three SDKs, three idioms (Python decorators + type hints + asyncio; TS generics + async/await + npm; Rust traits + async fn + cargo), one protocol, identical durability semantics. A workflow author does not know which language served an activity. It must feel native in each language while all three speak the exact same conversation with the engine, defined once in aion-proto.
